Name: Spotify playlist for every XXXTENTACION song on streaming.

CD Mixtape
(Mona Lisa)
Notes:
(23/01/1998) (Jahseh Onfroy is born)
(07/11/2013) (X plans to release the first track off the mixtape)
(11/02/2014) (The first song off of XXX UNMASTERED is released)
Streaming: The CD Mixtape is X’s earliest known project, being developed in early 2013 and allegedly being released on X’s Facebook mid 2013, and being uploaded to CD’s. This tape was frequently talked about on X’s Twitter by X and his older friend, J $oul. The tape supposedly featured around 10-12 tracks on it, The tape is boom bap based, featuring X rapping with 90s influence and a hint of Jamaican.

Name:
News
(feat. ???) (prod. Mixla)
Notes:
OG Filename: Track02 1.cda
"News" is the early version of the first part of "News/Flock" that was up on Soundcloud for only just three days. Was the very first song uploaded to X's SoundCloud account June 14th, 2013 (People still believe "vice city" is). Used the first instrumental heard in the finished "News/Flock" which is titled "MOBB DEEP UNDERGROUND HIP HOP DARK INSTRUMENTAL" by Mixla . Song potentially has early, possibly unfinalized lyrics and also contains the same feature that would appear in the final version of News/Flock.

Name:
⭐ News/Flock
(feat. ???) (prod. Mixla, Homi, TheOriginalBeat & Tyler, The Creator)
(Yard Man Ting)
Notes: Features an unknown man singing in Jamaican dialect. Before resurfacing after a groupbuy for $4,500 that began on November 13th, 2019, many fakes were made. The song finally leaked on November 15th, 6 years after its initial release. Recorded in a “studio” and contains a beat switch, the later being a remix of Odd Future's "Oldie". Lyrics are not the same as his BET freestyle as previously thought. Was at one point planned to be in the documentary released in 2022.

Name:
Ruff neck (snippet)
(prod. Wreckx-N-Effect, Aqil Davidson, Markell Riley & Walter ‘Mucho’ Scott)
Notes: "Ruff neck (snippet)" Is a 2013 lost X song that most likely used the MC Lyte beat of the same name "Ruff Neck". It is unknown if a full version was developed or if the song was only made as a snippet. The cover art for the song was lost but allegedly used his profile at the time. Was deleted April 4th, 2014

Name: 1998 (acapella)
Notes: "1998 (acapella)" is a lost acapella track X uploaded to Soundcloud and also performed to his friends at the time with an alleged Earl Sweatshirt like flow. A version with a beat could have been made over "Twinz" by Big Pun & Fat Joe but like most X info this is just a rumour.

Name: 21 (acapella)
Notes: 21 (acapella) is another lost acapella upload that would actually appear on his partially lost "XXX (UNMASTERED)" mixtape. In X's words, this acapella was about being arrested and his subsequent experience in jail. Unlike 1998, this track was most likely never intended to have an instrumental.

Name: the end is nigh *dirty* *acapella*
Notes: "the end is nigh *dirty* *acapella*" is the second to final acapella Soundcloud upload on X's page and is also lost like the rest of them. The acapella track is most likely an early acapella version of "THE END IS NIGH" and is the earliest known lost from 2014. Deleted a month after its initial upload.

Name:
XXX (UNMASTERED)
(XXX *unfinished*)
Notes:
(11/02/2014) (The first song off of XXX UNMASTERED is released)
(06/03/2014) (The final track for XXX UNMASTERED is released)
Streaming: X’s debut mixtape, XXX mainly consisted of his earlier songs from 2013 and 2014 on SoundCloud. “Vice City,” “al.one,” “M011Y,” “DEAD” and “THE END IS NIGH” were supposedly on the project but he ended up putting other songs he made instead. It has since been privated and a lot of songs were lost, but "Vice City”, X’s oldest public song on his SoundCloud to this day, survived. More than half of the mixtape is leaked. The full version can’t be found anywhere on the Internet. The tracklisting itself is also unknown.

Name:
SDS *Snippet
(feat. Nyora Spouse) (prod. Flying Lotus)
Notes: Features old-time collaborator Nyora Spouse, who has stated the song is very hard, though an old tweet from Vlone mentioned his part was trash and "can't rap" regarding contributions on the song. A remix of Mac Miller's song of the same name. The song is in fact a freestyle and it's unknown if a full version was ever made.

Name:
Nyora Spouse - SDS Snippet [Upload 2]
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Flying Lotus)
Notes: Upload of "SDS *Snippet" on Nyora's SoundCloud account. This upload was deleted within a day. Unknown if the upload was shorter or longer. Cover art was most likely the exact same but we dont know for certain.

Name:
✨Whores On The Boards
(feat. Nyora Spouse) (prod. Kanye West, Don Cannon & 88-Keys)
Notes:
OG Filename: whores on the boards.mp3
Also features Nyora Spouse and acts as a sister song to "SDS *Snippet", as both were posted on the same day and shared the same explicit cover art. Was planned since 2013 and is a remix of Pusha T's song "Numbers On The Boards". It was privately sold and leaked by Shogani on April 10th, 2020 following a failed buy days before.

Name:
$l0w
(feat. Nyora Spouse) (prod. ???)
Notes: Apart from containing another Nyora feature, not much is known about this song but X mentioning it on his twitter saying it was not his, though it was still uploaded to his SoundCloud. Does not share the same explicit art as X and Nyora's two other collaborations uploaded on this day. Part of the throwaways for the Nyora's first project "Tensaki".

Name:
Palm Trees
(feat. Jajfaru & Nyora Spouse) (prod. ???)
Notes:
OG Filename: Palm Trees.wav
Features Jajfaru, would who later go on to be known as "MUUUDY BOOTS", and Nyora Spouse. A chill remix of the Flatbush Zombies song of the same name. Bought off of Nyora in 2018 and subsquently leaked by 99. Was the last X song to be leaked while he was still alive.

Name:
Nyora Spouse - $LUMPED WHIP (SNIPPET UNMASTERED)
(feat. NewAge & XXXTENTACION) (prod. Kidrio)
Notes: One of X's first features, alongside NewAge (who would later go on to be known as "Sully The Chief God"). A snippet of the full "$LUMPED WHIP" and was planned to appear on Nyora's first project "Tensaki". It's unknown if the upload was of the acapella or with the instrumental, though we have both. The snippet itself is lost.

Name:
Nyora Spouse - Breathe Slow
(feat. XXXTENTACION & EDOSE) (prod. ???)
Notes: Another X feature alongside EDose (whose identity is currently unknown). Allegedly uses the instrumental of Lil B's song of the same name (TYBG). Was discovered alongside "$LUMPED WHIP" and features the same cover art of both the unmastered snippet and Nyora Spouse's project "Tensaki". This track is most likely the full version of $l0w that X had posted earlier.

Name:
✨ M011Y
(prod. DJ PHATAALI)
Notes:
OG Filename: jahsay.mp3
Was originally thought to have been an unreleased og version of the song until the link to songs upload was found. Uses a DJ PHATALLI beat and acts a an early version of "M011Y *FREESTYLE* *SLOPPED*" with no chops or change in speed. Strangely, this upload uses the same cover art as "vice city" but in much lower quality and before "vice city" would even release. Leaked from a groupbuy for $2,500 on July 14th, 2020. The song samples Fuck You Hoes by Frayser Click and Satasen laina by Nylon Beat.

Name:
⭐ GANG CYPHER *FREESTYLE*
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God, E BOOGIE, NewAge & Nyora Spouse) (prod. Spencer Bellamy)
Notes:
OG Filename: cypher.mp3
Features X's friends from the time, including eventual frequent collaborator Ski Mask The Slump God, E BOOGIE (who would later go on to be known as Au Flexgod), NewAge (Sully The Chief God), and Nyora Spouse. Is a remix over the "Shady Cypher 2.0". Purchased through a groupbuy for $1,350 and leaked on April 4th, 2020 at 11:55 PM EST. Is X's longest available song and the first X & Ski Mask The Slump God collaboration, the first of many. Was thought to be a different song as the consensus before was that the freestyle was recorded over Mobb Deep's "Shook Ones, Pt. II", when in actuality was over the "Shady Cypher 2.0". Was recorded in the Piper High School hallway and released the same day. A picture of the recording is public.

Name: Jehovah *Freestyle* *Acapella*
Notes: An acapella freestyle made in February with not much known about it. One current theory is that this an acapella upload of "M011Y" due to the fact that both tracks are freestyles and X mentions Jehovah in "M011Y", the only time in his discography. This is pure speculation and has not been proven.

Name:
🏆 al.one
(prod. COKEY)
Notes: Not to be confused with "Alone" from late 2014, "al.one" has since been proven to be a completely different song that is still lost. The proof for this distinction is that producer Cokey only sent X stolen trap/rap beats at the time, with the entirety of "XXX (UNMASTERED)" being over boom bap instrumentals. Furthermore, X only ever started mentioning "The Fall" and experimenting with more emo & atmospheric sounds near the end of the year. Was unmastered and a rushed release, leading to a distorted bass. Acts as the intro track to the "XXX (UNMASTERED)" mixtape.

Name:
⭐vice city
(prod. Canis Major)
Notes:
OG Filename: vice city.wav
Oldest song currently public on X's SoundCloud and was re-released onto streaming in January of 2022, eventually acting as the intro track to the "LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM" that accompanied the documentary of the same name. Is the only song off of "XXX (UNMASTERED)" that has not been deleted. Contains ear-peircing Hi Hats.

Name:
🏆 THE END IS NIGH
(prod. ???)
Notes: One of X's favorite early works. Typical boom bap song allegedly. Over the 47 Goonz instrumental. Up until April 4, 2014. Was believed to be originally intended for X’s Mona Lisa album under its original name “47 Goonz," but there's nothing to prove that. Was released as a part of his spring 2014 project XXX (UNMASTERED).

Name:
M011Y *FREESTYLE* *SLOPPED*
(prod. YXXXNZ & DJ PHATAALI)
Notes:
OG Filename: molly.mp3
Originally recorded unslopped, X eventually told long-time producer YXXXNZ (also known as just "yan" and "YVN" on the slopped upload) to remix both the vocals and instrumental. This version of the song leaked on December 16, 2017. The original, unslopped version of the song with one extra unheard line was sold on July 5, 2020 and eventually bought and leaked for $2,500 on July 14, 2020, six years after both versions' releases. A teaser for this song was released on Twitter on February 27, 2014.

Name:
✨DEAD
(prod. ItsGoodBeats)
Notes:
OG Filename: DEAD.wav
X stated he listened to this on repeat and that it was a bonus track on the "XXX (UNMASTERED)" mixtape. An account with the name "Devil" uploaded the song to SoundCloud on January 23rd, 2017, making it public once again. OG file was leaked by Mitchfek after 2 years. Very lyrical.

Name: Ice Hotel
Notes:
(06/03/2014) (The final track for XXX UNMASTERED is released)
(07/04/2014) (ICE HOTEL EP is released)
Streaming: XXXTENTACION’s second EP Ice Hotel, highlights some of his rarer material that is unavailable to most of the general public. Like most of his early tracks, it was said to have been deleted from SoundCloud for an unknown reason that X never revealed. but it was later discovered that the EP was privated. Ranging from somber tracks such as “0C3AN” and “Ice Hotel” to ominous ones such as “FUCK V2” and “sounds of the melting pot”, X’s versatility can be noted throughout the 8-track EP.

Name:
✨ DUTTY
(prod. Canis Major)
Notes:
OG Filename: dutty.wav
Another very lyrical track using a deleted Canis Major beat. X embraces his Jamaican heritage and dialect. Longtime friend and producer Mitchfek reuploaded the song to SoundCloud on October 5th, 2018, making it public once again. Prior to its resurfacing, it was thought to be a snippet for either "(VERYRAREBOYZ)" or "vice city".

Name:
✨ (VERYRAREBOYZ)
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God & Drugz) (prod. Kaieffin Beatz)
Notes: Features Drugz (who would later go on to be known as Zilla and eventually Lil RedDot) and longtime collaborator Ski Mask The Slump God. Uses a stolen Tyler, The Creator type beat and acts as the signature song of the quartet (also including FUCKABITCHFACE) group of the same name. Appears to have been recorded with all three present. Uses flow similar to the song "DEAD". Resurfaced when old-time producer Mitchfek reuploaded the entirety of "ICE HOTEL EP" to his Datpiff account on May 11th, 2015. Appears as track five on "ICE HOTEL EP".

Name:
DUTTY [Upload 2]
(prod. Canis Major)
Notes:
OG Filename: dutty.wav
A reupload of "DUTTY" removing the "& XXX" production credit from the upload title. Unknown if there were any differences between the uploads other than it maybe having a different cover art..

Name:
FUCK V2
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. Computofresh)
Notes: Features Ski Mask The Slump God and acts as a joke diss towards Nyora Spouse. Acts as the seventh and final track on "ICE HOTEL EP". X's Verse is very low quality.  The cover art of the song is a picture of Nyora Spouse in elementary school. The song samples No One's Gonna Love You by The S.O.S. Band which was also sampled by A$AP Rocky & A$AP Ty Beats in their hit track "Peso".

Name:
Blood Stains
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God & Drugz) (prod. Sqvxlls)
Notes: Features Ski Mask The Slump God and Drugz and contains a darker tone set by the eerie reverberations of the Playstation 2 start up sample, complimenting the haunting and vulgar lyrics. Appears as track four on "ICE HOTEL EP". Song uses the same instrumental as YUNGXANHOE by Playboi Carti.

Name:
🏆 HUNNID
(prod. MYRROR)
Notes: “HUNNID” is a now-deleted track that was uploaded to X’s Soundcloud in March of 2014 and is produced by  MYRROR who would later produce "OC3AN *clip*" and "I&M". The track’s existence was found in a tweet from close-friend Ski Mask the Slump God, mentioning the track. Ski also mentioned about making a remix of the song, it is currently unknown if it was recorded. It has been recently confirmed that the track was a written song, not a freestyle. The track likely uses an exclusive MYRROR beat. Was taken down after around a week alongside "ICY".

Name:
✨ 0C3AN *clip*
(prod. MYRROR)
Notes: Shares sample with "INUYASHA" that features a police chase that ended in a suicide that was broadcasted on live television. A full version featuring Zay $oul (later known as Coolie Pak) exists. Appears as track three on "ICE HOTEL EP".

Name:
🏆 ICY
(prod. ???)
Notes: One of the most unknown and obscure X tracks of all time, making it a grail within the community. allegedly uses SpaceGhostPurrp's "Icy" instrumental. Though its title may suggest otherwise, it does not appear anywhere on "ICE HOTEL EP".

Name:
Ski Mask The Slump God - Felonies
(prod. XXXTENTACION)
Notes: A Ski Mask The Slump God track that X had produced and uploaded to his account. It is possible that this track is Ski Mask The Slump God's first solo song "Catch Me" as Ski Mask The Slump God uploaded it with "prod. XXX" in the title, and the phrase "Catch me" can be somewhat relevant to committing felonies.

Name:
✨ Zay $oul - Kick In The Door
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. DJ Premier)
Notes:
OG Filename: 4Kick In The Door.mp3
X's feature on Zay $oul's (Coolie Pak) song. A remix of The Notorious B.I.G.'s famous 1997 hit of the same name. X's verse in particular contains very strange and controversial lyrics, reminiscent of early Slim Shady Eminem. Short snippets of his verse existed for years, at one point making this song a grail. Was groupbought for $1,242 and leaked on September 17th, 2019. Both a snippet and freestyle were uploaded to Zay $oul's SoundCloud prior to the final product, though it is likely that X was on neither. OG File for the song leaked after the "The Last Dance" groupbuy which would get cancelled after $8,000 would be raised on February 5th 2026.

Name:
🏆 cum
(feat. Lxuisavage & Spooky Leann) (prod. Terio & SpaceGhostPurrp)
Notes: Features both LxuiSavage and Spooky Lean who claimed to have this song multiple times, but ended up scamming each time money was involved. Lxuisavage doesn’t know where this song can be located. A grail in the community that little is known about, though it uses the instrumental from SpaceGhostPurrp's "Cum & Git Yuh Some". Was up for just three weeks. Countless fake snippets have been made and both features have been known to scam people for the song. Was at one point considered for "ICE HOTEL EP" due to the song's description but was ultimately left off for unknown reasons. Screenshot of the song on SoundCloud during that time is available. We currently have all the lyrics to the song as they were leaked May 30th 2024 by Vlone.

Name:
Ski Mask The Slump God - Catch Me
(prod. LORENZOSTAYRICH & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: catch me.mp3
“Catch Me” is Ski Mask's first ever solo song uploaded to SoundCloud. It was deleted on October 29th, 2015 and was a lost track until it was leaked on September 3rd, 2018 by MitchFek, a close friend of X and Ski who had many losts of them. X helped record and produce this song.

Name:
⭐️ INUYASHA
(feat. Drugz) (prod. Bitoy Beatz)
Notes:
OG Filename: INUYASHA.wav
Features Drugz (Zilla, Lil Red Dot). Shares same live suicide sample as "0C3AN *clip*". Being one of X's earliest aggressive tracks, this was publically available until 2016 and remains private on X's SoundCloud to this day, though the link has since reset. Appears as track two on "ICE HOTEL EP". Bitoy Beatz also produced a lot of early LUCKI songs.

Name:
ICE HOTEL (intro)
(feat. SZA) (prod. Sir CRKS, OfficialCDCN, WondaGurl & XXXTENTACION)
Notes: Recorded over a chopped & screwed version of SZA's "Teen Spirit", done by SIR CRKS, And a second instrumental by OfficialCDCN for X's verse. A longer, more drawn out song, X had publically expressed his personal hate for this song, later taking it down in 2016. The song would eventually find itself on SZA's SoundCloud on March 11th, 2018. It is worth noting the initial upload title read "RZA", not "SZA", before being subsequently changed. It is unknown why this happened. Acts as the title and intro track to "ICE HOTEL EP".

Name: e.motion
Notes:
(07/04/2014) (The final track for ICE HOTEL is released)
(10/07/2014) (X tweets out "THE NOBODYS" for the first time)
Streaming: The information on the “e.motion EP”, is extremely scarce. The only information left behind was its title, cover, and the song “Ecstasy”. The info was hidden within the depths of the internet until September 28th, 2022, when Discord user shots! discovered it.

Name:
⭐ 3 AM freestyle
(prod. Clams Casino)
Notes: “3 AM freestyle” serves as an apology track to Ski Mask The Slump God, after the two had an undisclosed altercation that resulted in X being kicked out of Ski’s birthday party. The track is also a diss to several people, specifically Nyora Spouse, Yung Grabba, Yung Gook and the group PC. X raps over a pitched down version of the legendary Clams Casino & Imogen Heap instrumental titled “I’m God” which has been used by a handful of rappers. The song originally released on April 19, 2014, with the track being deleted and lost for nearly seven years. Was uploaded twice and was one of the biggest grails in the community before leaking for free and unexpectedly on March 2nd, 2021 by a Discord user named Vise. The track was also co signed by Clams Casino himself when it leaked which could lead to the song being official cleared at some point.

Name:
⭐ ecstasy
(feat. Anneka) (prod. XXYYXX)
Notes:
OG Filename: ecstacy.wav
Original version of the song that would later appear on X's second & final posthumous album entitled "Bad Vibes Forever", with the majority of Anekka's vocals replaced with Noah Cyrus's. X raps over XXYYXX's "Overdone", a remix of Anekka's acapella track "Shut Her Down". Was deleted almost a year after its upload. The song is also the ONLY known track on "e.motion" EP with its title being in the song's description. Was groupbought for $3,000 in September 2019.

Name:
🏆 hoe
(prod. Rednarf)
Notes: A grail in the community, alleged to be a diss track towards either X's mother or his ex. Images of X's DM's with Ski at the time show that he wanted HIM to be on the song, though it's clear through the upload title he never recorded for it. It is unknown whether or not he wrote a verse. X stated multiple times his flow on the song was "crazy". The cover art comes from a porno.

Name:
3 AM (freestyle) [Upload 2]
(prod. Clams Casino)
Notes: A reupload on May 14th. Unknown if they were any differences between the two uploads other than one having "freestyle" in brackets in the title and them having different cover arts with this uploads cover art being the green image of X.

Name:
🏆 ok (freestyle)
(prod. ???)
Notes: A freestyle that is labeled to be fast and aggressive by X's friends who heard it while it was public. According to twitter dm's between X and Ski the song is a diss towards his ex girlfriend "Destiney" who slept with X's friend and he found out during Ski's birthday party which led to him going into a fit of rage leading him to be kicked out which led to disses like "3 AM (freestyle)" and "hoe" being made. We know it contains a beat switch from a tweet talking about the song. ok - snail

With "ICY" as being the most obscure X song ever. There is nothing known about this track besides its cover art, and it's possible that it's a snippet for a song, a full song itself, a sample for a song, or simply testing SoundCloud's copyright system. Its cover art shares a similar design with "catch" which was a remix of a Drake song.

Name:
🏆 elegant
(prod. Kyle Stemberger & cight)
Notes: A partial grail as we now have an extended snippet showcasing most of the song. Had multiple alleged instrumentals with cight (Edward Skeletrix) & Kyle Stemberger's "Ｚｅｎ ａｎｄ Ｌｅａｎ" being the official instrumental and is said to be owned by Cleo and the label. Was recorded in a studio, where the snippet originally came from via instagram @train_line_dub_agency

Name:
tokyo nostalgia
(prod. KayKayOnTheBeats)
Notes: A 2022 discovered lost where X likely stole the instrumental from producer KayKayOnTheBeats which was found by uleft (goon). Not much is known outside the instrumental and cover art, which its origins are lost.

Name:
✨ sounds of the melting pot
(prod. HYDRO)
Notes:
OG Filename: Sounds Of The Melting Pot.mp3
A chill song containing many samples and heavy bass. The cover art was eventually changed to scrapped mixtape "THE NOBODYS" a month or two after the song's release. An eventual remix was made by longtime producer YXXXNZ (or simply yan). Appears as track six on "ICE HOTEL EP" and is most likely on "THE NOBODYS" mixtape because of its artwork change.

Name:
🏆 john carpenter
(feat. The Weeknd) (prod. Silkky Johnson)
Notes: “John Carpenter” is a lost X song uploaded on the 8th of June 2014. It is currently the longest known X track with over 7 minutes in length, it allegedly uses the “Kiss Land” instrumental by well known R&B artist The Weeknd as The Weeknd would upload a snippet of his song "Kiss Land" onto his YouTube with the title "John Carpenter", who X often looked up to as inspiration. It is said that this track was removed off SoundCloud over copyright, even if X changed and distorted the instrumental.

Name:
✨ catch
(prod. 40 & JaZa Edits)
Notes: Remix of the second half of Drake's song "0 To 100 / The Catch Up", with backing vocals from James Blake. Was up until 2016, when X privated it on his SoundCloud. The track was subsequently deleted in 2017 when X and Drake had beef. The track would later resurface when warrenlindstrom would post the track on his Soundcloud but would be chopped up weirdly.

Name:
Bluddy Katon freestyle
(prod. Purpdogg)
Notes:
OG Filename: bluddy katon.wav
X's first noticeably well-mixed song. Shares the same instrumental with Robb $tone's "Chill Bill", leading people to believe X's song was a diss toward Rob, though its inception came long before the two artist's beef with one another in the latter half of X's career. A longer version of the song exists, five minutes in length, to which a 15-second video snippet of the recording process exists, posted to Fat Mike's Instagram the day of the song's creation and reposted again by old-time collaborator Sully The Chief God on June 18th, 2019, one year after X's death.

Name:
✨ Who The Fuck Is God
(feat. Sully The Chief God & AbsentWill) (prod. NSD)
Notes:
OG Filename: who the fuck is god.wav
Features Sully The Chief God (first known as NewAge) and X's old manager AbsentWill credited as the "Random NIgga" in the upload title. X's verse details his experience of finding his uncle who committed suicide via hanging, and was later re-recorded for the track "Angels 2 Demons (Daemons)". Is still available on Fat Mike's SoundCloud to this day.

Name: THE NOBODYS
Notes:
(10/07/2014) (X tweets out "THE NOBODYS" for the first time)
(16/08/2014) (The final track for "THE NOBODYS is released)
Streaming: Often mistaken to be a collaborative project between X, Ski Mask, and producer YXXXNZ, "THE NOBODYS" mixtape was released in July 2014 before being updated once. The only confirmed tracks are uploads with the tape's cover and title being used on the songs' cover arts and descriptions. Later mentions of "THE NOBODYS" most likely refer to x, ski, and yan as a collective rather than a project.

Name:
🏆 The shuttle
(prod. ???)
Notes: A relatively unknown song that was on the NOBODYS project, known through its cover art and description. A fake was "leaked" through the fan project "XXXTRA RARE TRACKS, VOL. 1" titled "THE SHUTTLE INTERLUDE" and plenty fake snippets have been made since then.

Name:
NOBODYS (intro)
(prod. No Doubt, Matthew Wilder & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: NOBODYS (intro).wav
The intro and title track to the scrapped "THE NOBODYS" mixtape. A 30-second song sampling a 1997 performance of No Doubt's hit trackk "Dont Speak". Was leaked by (the goat) Bountry on July 4th, 2017, with it's private link found later on March 25th, 2020. The track was also featured on "XXXTRA RARE TRACKS VOL. 1" along with the fake version of the "the shuttle".

Name:
✨ Ski Mask The Slump God - zones
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Cosmastly & FALCO)
Notes:
OG Filename: ski mask the slump god - zones feat. xxxtentacion.wav
An X feature, the longest X & Ski collab currently known. Very laid back and atmospheric. Is only available today because Ski enabled it to be downloaded off his SoundCloud account and encouraged people on Twitter to do so before deleting it. Was subsequently leaked by user xoxoanyone on a SoundCloud repost page. Shares a "Mario Kart 64" Toad scream with X's solo "sounds of the melting pot".

Name:
sounds of the melting pot (YXXXNZ TWIST)
(prod. HYDRO & YXXXNZ)
Notes:
OG Filename: sounds of the melting pot 3.mp3
Chopped & screwed version of the track done by longtime producer YXXXNZ (yan) that features a sample of cum's instrumental. After countless fakes, (goat) Vlone leaked the true song on March 21st, 2021.

Name:
✨ captain save a hoe
(feat. ziLLA) (prod. The Dark Side & Teflon)
Notes:
OG Filename: captain save a hoe.wav
Features Zilla (first known as Drugz, later known as Lil Red Dot). A fast paced lyrical track with whispering vocals. Resurfaced on July 5th, 2017 through X's subreddit, appearing on the unofficial "XXXTRA RARE TRACKS, VOL. 1".

Name:
✨ ghost in the shell
(feat. Nyora Spouse) (prod. FALCO)
Notes:
OG Filename: Ghost In The Shell.wav
Features Nyora with arguably his best verse and is the original, unpitched version of "ghosts" that X uploaded & swiftly deleted off SoundCloud for unknown reasons. Perhaps he realized it was the wrong file, though this is pure speculation.

Name:
ghosts
(feat. Nyora Spouse)  (prod. FALCO)
Notes:
OG Filename: ghosts2.mp3
The final chopped & screwed version of "ghost in the shell". Resurfaced through X's subreddit, appearing on the unofficial "XXXTRA RARE TRACKS, VOL. 1". Confirmed to be on the scrapped "THE NOBODYS" mixtape through its cover art and description.

Name: The Fall
Notes:
(16/08/2014) (The final track for THE NOBODYS is released)
(28/11/2014) (The final track for The Fall is released)
Streaming: As one of Florida rapper, XXXTENTACION’s earlier EPs, The Fall exemplifies some of X’s more rarer tracks. It consists of only five songs and showcases some of his versatility, ranging from somber songs such as “NEVER” and “The Fall” to more ominous, trap based ones like “FUCKABITCHFACE” and “white girl".

Name:
J $oul - Angels 2 Demons
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. ChansliN)
(Daemons)
Notes: An alternate version of "Who The Fuck Is God" by X's old friend J $oul with a featured re-recorded X verse. This song's existence was not known until a link on Twitter was found. Two more versions of this song have been made and released: one with a different, newer J $oul verse (updated post-X's death) that was uploaded by J $oul on his SoundCloud, and one on X's second & final posthumous album, Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Joey Bada$$ & Kemba, simply entitled "Daemons". Was planned to leak alongside the Zay $oul (unrelated) collaboration "Kick In The Door", but leaked prior due to complications in the buy.

Name:
ivebeentohelllucifersastandupguy
(feat. ya$) (prod. ???)
Notes: A newly discovered lost that features and was harmonized by X's old friend Yasmine (credited as "ya$"). Its existence was discovered on September 28th, 2022 by user Shots! among some other songs. Its title bears some resemblance to the popular "I spoke to the devil in miami", though is in no way directly related beyond the name. The track also had some tweets about it but were deleted.

Name:
✨ OKAGE THE SHADOW KING
(prod. GF Retro)
Notes:
OG Filename: OKAGE THE SHADOW KING.wav
One of X’s deleted songs from his SoundCloud, on a beat sampling “Sacred Grounds – Esto Gaza,” from the Final Fantasy IX soundtrack. Though one of X’s older songs, it remains arguably one of his most lyrical ones, and its brevity (only an introduction and one verse) resembles many of X’s 2014-16 releases. Was deleted on April 12th, 2015.

Name:
🏆 WHO SHOT YA
(prod. Diddy, Nashiem Myrick & Poke)
Notes: A grail in the com, remixing The Notorious B.I.G.'s "Who Shot Ya". The second time X remixed a Biggie Smalls song, the first being "Kick In The Door". Allegedly very lyrical, and X has personally expressed his fond for the track. The cover art was his SoundCloud account picture at the time.

Name:
⭐ NEVER
(prod. Aesthesys)
Notes:
OG Filename: NEVER FINISHED.wav
One of X's signature songs. A very soft & mellow track with dark & depressive themes over a beautiful composition by Aesthesys. Is often cited as one of X's best songs. Part of "The Fall" EP, the fourth & final track on the 2016 EP "WILLY WONKA WAS A CHILD MURDERER", and appears on "LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM" as track two, released in 2022 to coincide with the documentary of the same name. Composer Aesthesys has stated he planned on suing X over the stealing of his work, though dropped the suit following X's death in June of 2018.

Name:
i wanna be a witch (cover)
(feat. Gabby Wade) (prod. Gabby Wade)
Notes: A recently discovered lost alongside "ivebeentohelllucifersastandupguy" among others. Likely a mellow song with X singing over Gabby Wade's cover of Teen Suicide's song "I Wanna Be A Witch".

Name:
✨ white girl
(prod. Kid Indigo & BLVC SVND)
Notes:
OG Filename: white.wav
“white girl” is the fourth song from X’s EP The Fall, it uses a stolen BLVC SVND beat. The song shows the happier and oblivious side of The Fall. Samples "You Again" by Kate Havnevik and is very distorted. Part of "The Fall" EP.

Name:
✨ FUCKABITCHFACE
(prod. Kid Indigo)
Notes:
OG Filename: FUCKABITCHFACE.wav
A tribute song to X's friend of the same name who was in the quartet group veryrareboyz alongside Zilla (Drugz) and Ski Mask. Very distorted and loud with X talking about his desire for sex and drugs. Part of "The Fall" EP.

Name:
Cadillac Boys - Mega Man
(feat. XXXTENTACION & Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. Kid Indigo)
Notes: X and Ski Mask The Slump God collab with Cadillac Boys. Is often reuploaded without the Cadillac Boys and was deleted twice, once in 2017 and again in 2018 with a music video intended to be shot. Uses Crystals by Kid Indigo as an instrumental.

Name:
pistol (snippet)
(prod. ROBSON)
Notes:
OG Filename: PISTOL.wav
Often confused to be the Vinny Chango$ remix with the intense 808 drop, which released a year later. This version of the song does not have a beat drop & was intended to appear on AU Flexgod's album, though he apparently never recorded his verse for the song. The solo X snippet was found on VK by user Dr. Lamentation on June 3rd, 2018.

Name:
🏆 brat bug (snippet)
(prod. GF Retro)
Notes: One of the biggest grails in the com, Was an aggressive, fast-paced song that allegedly had deep vocals according to someone who has allegedly heard it. X supposedly screams "Brat Bug" on the chorus which is just a rumor. Ended like how "RUN UP ON ME" ended where the audio faded during the song, said by a lead. Interestingly, in 2015 when X wanted to know what people wanted more from his songs a fan asked him to finish brat bug calling it "BUGS" in February 2015. Is less than a minute in length.

Name:
⭐ The Fall
(prod. Eera)
Notes:
OG Filename: the fall x.mp3
The title and intro track to "The Fall" EP. Is chopped and mixed extremely well. “The Fall” is about the lowest points in one’s life and the desperation that can come from these times. X says that many people, including himself, feel like their entire lives are a struggle and they will never be able to escape these difficult times.

Name:
✨ ghost
(prod. surrenderdorothy & GREAF)
Notes: Not to be confused with "ghosts" featuring Nyora, this is a somber track that appears on ''The Fall'' EP. It was written while X was in jail and describes the self-harm and numb aspect of ''The Fall'' . Deleted April 12th, 2015. We see the depressed side of X more in this song. He is saying that he feels so dead inside that his flesh is cold, like a dead body.

Name:
🏆 autumn burns bright
(prod. ???)
Notes: A recently discovered lost by user Shots!, possibly appearing on or scrapped from "The Fall" EP due to its cover art and description. Deleted on December 29th, 2014. Likely used a stolen beat, similar to "The Fall" and "NEVER" and is theorized to be the bandcamp exclusive song "blasted", though no definitive proof has been discovered. Interestingly, the only time he said this name was on his first 2015 collaboration with Zay $oul, "Pab Moob", near the end of the song. This song is definitely not an early version of Pab Moob though, since this was months before the collaboration took place.

Name: ♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡
Notes:
(28/11/2014) (The final track for The Fall is released)
(01/12/2014) (X releases all 3 tracks for ♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡)
Streaming: Released in late 2014, ♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡ is one of X’s darkest EP’s from the time with constant depressing themes, featuring one of X’s most arguably brutal tracks “rare”. Unlike most of X’s other early projects, the playlist is still up on his SoundCloud page, although every track featured on the EP has been deleted except for “a message to tina belcher.”

Name:
⭐ alone
(prod. GREAF)
Notes:
OG Filename: alone fin.mp3
Is often cited as X's best song and is commonly referred to as "ALONE PART, 1" and confused with the lost "al.one" from "XXX (UNMASTERED)". Leaked by (the goat) Bountry on July 2nd, 2017. Acts as the first installment of the "Alone Trilogy", preceding both "ALONE PART 2" from 2014 and "ALONE, PART 3" from 2018, off X's second & final album to be released in his lifetime, "?", appearing as track two. Acts as the intro track to the "♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡" EP.

Name:
✨ rare
(prod. Fifty Grand)
Notes:
OG Filename: rare.mp3
One of X's most infamous songs that contains violent lyrics including the brutal "I beat the bitch like Ray Rice, she won't testify if you ask her" line. Was leaked by (the goat) Bountry on December 16th, 2017 and was eventually re-released to streaming services in 2022 on the documentary album, appearing as track three. Often referred to as "Rare, Part 1". Appears as track two the "♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡" EP.

Name:
✨ a message to tina belcher
(prod. Fifty Grand)
Notes:
OG Filename: a message to tina belcher.wav
Named after a character from the show "Bob's Burgers", this one of X's saddest songs, a spoken word about why he is still alive and what the meaning of life is. Uses a Fifty Grand instrumental that is still available on their SoundCloud today. Acts as the third and final track on the "♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡" EP and is the only song from the project still publically available on X's account (apart from the recent reupload of "rare"). Bandcamp upload featured a different cover art which was an image of Tina herself.

Name:
⭐ blasted
(prod. Fifty Grand & CH4RM)
Notes:
OG Filename: blasted.mp3 
Has X rapping over a remixed version of Fifty Grands track "The People You Love" created by GHXVLS (CH4RM). The track was discovered when "//Nobodys\\" leaked, where a remixed portion of the track was included. The full, unedited track was leaked by an old X fan named Bountry who bought it from producer and frequent collaborator YXXXNZ on December 16th, 2017. In Feburary of 2024 the track was discovered to have been released only on Bandcamp, along with rest of "♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡" .

Name:
⭐ ALONE PART 2
(prod. Lil Netzero)
(what i do when alone)
Notes:
OG Filename: what i do when alone.mp3
Hands down the darkest and most disturbing X song of all time. Another incredibly violent and dark track with disturbing lyrics about murder, cannibalism, and rape. Contains hidden vocals in the beginning. Still available on his SoundCloud to this day. Is part 2 in the "Alone" trilogy.

Name:
✨ R U N
(prod. GREAF)
Notes:
OG Filename: run.wav
A solemn track that appears to allude to a rape and samples a Kanye West interview on "The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Kimmel". Falsely known as ''Here'', which is the name of the Greaf beat that X stole for the song. Still available on his SoundCloud to this day. The cover art was a picture of a dress that X sent to one of his friends at the time but girlfriend in 2016, Geneva Ayala.

Name:
Cadillac Boys - BOOFPACK 
(prod. KurtisBased & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: gaydre and edick.wav
Chill trap song made by the Cadillac Boys and was produced by KurtisBased and was only mixed by X despite the title. Track is considered to one of X's rarest tracks since he only has a production credit.

Name:
YXXXNZ - //Nobodys\\
(feat. XXXTENTACION & Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. YXXXNZ, Fifty Grand, CH4RM, greaf & Kaieffin Beatz)
Notes:
OG Filename: Serato Recording 4.aif
A mix of various X & Ski Mask The Slump God tracks sent to YXXXNZ for him to mixIncluding; a message to tina belcher, LOW, rare, alone, blasted & (VERYRAREBOYZ) that released in 2014 and then later leaked in 2017 after being deleted. It provided us with a snippet of the unheard track "blasted", which was eventually found. Both X and YXXXNZ wanted the track to have a music video but was never recorded.

Name:
🏆 MEANY
(prod. ???)
Notes: Not much is known about this track apart from it being the final upload of 2014. Could be the full version of "I & M" since the OG file for that song reads "MEANY half".

Red Light District
(Heartbreak Hotel)
Notes:
(??/01/2015) (X begins to work on Red Light District.)
(22/02/2015) (X releases the final RLD track before scrapping the project.)
Streaming: This EP follows a dark theme, just like the other projects X released at the time. It is unclear how many tracks this EP would feature, yet we are sure that the six present tracks existed and were a part of it. Being one of X’s rarest and smaller projects, most of its tracks are extremely rare or lost; they will probably never be recovered.

Name:
ZAY $OUL - pab moob (snippet)
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. NICKFLYFL)
Notes: Snippet of pab moob with X featuring on it being unknown as there is 0 information about other than its existence. Was deleted January 15th, a day after the final version's upload, though it's unknown when it was uploaded.

Name:
ZAY $OUL - pab moob
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. NICKFLYFL)
Notes:
OG Filename: ###pab moob ft. xxxtentacion.wav
"pab moob" is Zay $oul's (Coolie Pak currently) song that contains the oldest X feature that has not been deleted. A boom bap track where X literally spits on the mic. Recorded sometime in late 2014 and samples a 50 Cent interview from 2010 in the intro. An interesting thing of note is that "pab moob" backwards is "boom bap".

Name:
rolling
(prod. YXXXNZ & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: random 8 ( jahsehs version ).wav
An instrumental collaboration between YXXXNZ & X that was uploaded for only a few hours. It was at one point in time a grail in the community, believed to contain vocals. The collaborated beat samples "Hold On, We're Going Home" by Drake. Was leaked alongside two other versions of the track, "Red Light Lie (Intro)" & and a ton of YXXXNZ beats May 8th, 2021 by (goat) Vlone.

Name:
ZAY $OUL - pab moob
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. NICKFLYFL)
Notes: A reupload of pab moob onto Bandcamp by Zay $oul (Coolie Pak), features 0 audio differences but has a different cover art used which is just static.

Name:
✨ Clarity (Snippet)
(prod. Sly C)
Notes:
OG Filename: clarity.wav
A snippet of another potental collaboration with Zay $oul (Coolie Pak) that was apparently recorded, though it's unknown if the full was ever uploaded. Samples a short segment from a 2013 interview with Gucci Mane.

Name:
⭐ skin
(prod. little cloud)
Notes:
OG Filename: xxx skin.wav
Another one of X's arguable greatest yet most depressing songs he created. An unmixed somber track about X murdering a guilt-ridden, suicidal love interest and the man she cheated with, before ultimately committing suicide himself. Is the only track in the ''Heartbreak Hotel'' playlist still available on his SoundCloud. A rumoured mixed version of the track supposedly exists but all signs lead to that being untrue. Uses ", tapeloop #1 ~winter 2008-2009" by ANGEL DIARY (little cloud) as an instrumental.

Name:
⭐ leave
(prod. little cloud)
Notes:
OG Filename: xxx leave.wav
The only other confirmed song to be on "Heartbreak Hotel" alongside "skin". X felt that the song needed "to be improved", so he deleted it 20 minutes after its upload. He never reuploaded the track, "leaving" it to become a grail within the community. At one point the song was thought to be a self-produced beat by X before it was discovered it was produced by ANGEL DIARY and contained vocals. It finally leaked exactly one year after X's passing by Kilo Jr., a friend of X's whose house he recorded both "skin" & "leave" at. Kilo found the file on his computer and sold it for $800, before leaking it through users Bren & **. The audio is unmixed and extremely loud, though a mixed supposedly version exists.

Name:
red light lie (intro)
(prod. YXXXNZ & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: red light lie (intro).m4a
A short intro for the 2015 project "Red Light District". Discovered and leaked by Vlone through YXXXNZ email. Interestingly this and probably every upload using the red cover used was the same cover since "ivebeentohelllucifersastandupguy". X had sent producer YXXXNZ the bass line to this. Leaked by Vlone on March 21st, 2021.

Name:
"The Devils Playground" Red Light Lie (clip)
(prod. YXXXNZ)
Notes:
OG Filename: red light lie.wav
Later reuploaded as simply "Red", which for the longest time was assumed to be a gruesome, violent song. Eventually it was discovered that both "The Devils Playground" and "Red" shared the same file, proving the later to be a reupload of the former. Is completely original with no samples used.

Name:
✨ Love (Interlude)
(prod. BYOU$)
Notes:
OG Filename: Love (interlude).wav
The interlude from the scrapped project "Red Light District". In July 2019, Discord server “XXX & SKI GB HUB” began a group buy for the track. The song reached over $1,100 in just a few hours, leading to the song being leaked on July 17th, 2019. It is possible X wrote this song in relation to his ex Cinthia, releasing the interlude around Valentine's day. The instrumental samples Marilyn Manson's "If I was Your Vampire". The song didnt have a cover or just wasnt cached.

Name:
Red
(prod. YXXXNZ)
Notes:
OG Filename: red light lie.wav
A reupload of "The Devils Playground", which for the longest time was assumed to be a gruesome, violent song. Eventually it was discovered that both "The Devils Playground" and "Red" shared the same file, proving the latter to be a reupload of the former. Is completely original with no samples used.

Name:
Members Only Vol. 1
(Collaboration with Ski Mask The Slump God)
Notes:
(22/02/2015) (X releases the final RLD track before scrapping the project.)
(20/04/2015) (X releases Members Only Vol. 1.)
Streaming: XXXTENTACION and Ski Mask The Slump God joined forces to create the Members Only rap collective and their first ever release. The two artists still displayed their versatility on this seven-track project as the material ranged from a classic hip-hop approach on tracks such as “Freddy vs. Jason” to heavier tracks like “FUXK” and “GXD DAMN” which would soon morph into X’s signature style. Noticeably, like much of X and Ski’s earlier material, all of the tracks on Members Only Vol. 1 are self-produced with the exception of Willie G who assisted the duo in three of the seven tracks present in the mixtape.

Name:
Freddy vs Jason (Unmastered)
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. WILLIE G)
Notes: The unmastered version of the iconic X & Ski collaboration where they trade bars back-to-back about sex & murder. This also marks their first collaboration with producer WILLIE G. This particular version of the song is lost, though the final, mixed product is available & extremely popular in both artists' discographies. They once rapped this track to get out of a ticket.

Name:
✨ I & M
(prod. MYRROR)
Notes:
OG Filename: MEANY half.mp3
One of the most obscure X songs publically available that features a sped up performance over a MYRROR instrumental. This track is supposedly the half version of "MEANY" from late 2014 due to it's file name. The track is currently private on X's SoundCloud.

Name:
Nyora Spouse - PRIME TIME
(feat. XXXTENTACION & Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. InfamousLegends)
Notes:
OG Filename: PRIME TIME chop SPEED.wav
 Commonly referred to as "Sloppy Toppy", this is a collaboration between X, Ski, and Nyora that X wanted deleted, though it never was. According to X, this is the only song he has with Nyora that Nyora performs well on, though X himself & Ski set the bar so high with their verses that it's an "iffy thing". Has multiple versions with alternative Nyora vocals, though X & Ski never recorded anything more for this song, so their contributions are the same.

Name:
Prime (Verse)
(prod. InfamousLegends)
Notes: A solo upload of X's verse from "PRIME TIME" onto X's Soundcloud profile. Allegedly it had either unpitched or even extended vocals, though this is not confirmed. Track would later be deleted and would remain lost for over 10 years where it is still lost as of 2026

Name:
✨ RestInPussy!
(prod. WILLIE G)
Notes:
OG Filename: 04 RestInPussy.mp3
X rapping about sex and other violent tendencies with a very raspy and croaky voice over a WILLIE G beat which samples "Oridnary Vanity" by Akira Yamaoka from the Silent Hill 2 soundtrack. Is track 4 on Members Only Vol. 1 based on the file name being "04 RestInPussy.mp3"

Name:
⭐ FUXK
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. WILLIE G)
Notes:
OG Filename: fukk.wav
“FUXK” is a track from XXXTENTACION and Ski Mask the Slump God’s first project, Members Only, Vol.1!. It is the first song that brought serious attention to X and Ski: when asked in his No Jumper Interview when his “music started popping off on SoundCloud,” X responds “as soon as we dropped FUXK” (59:55). The song features a bass heavy instrumental with quick flows from Ski and aggressive rapping from X, albeit not the shouting style he would adopt later. “FUXK” was produced by Willie G, who also produced tracks off Members Only, Vol.1! The instrumental to each of these songs is available on Willie G’s youtube channel. The track was re-released on official streaming services on LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM on June 10, 2022

(prod. WILLIE G & Grouchy VanGogh)
(RUN UP ON ME)

Most commonly known by its second title, "RUN UP ON ME", this is a classic aggressive track from X that acts as a diss on Bukkweat Bill. Eventually the beef was squashed, leading to the change in title, cover art, and with x wanting to release the alleged full version but never releasing it or being leaked to this day.

Nyora & AleXander track with X is on adlibs throughout the track. Could possibly one of X's most unknown features as it was uncredited. The track is private on Nyora's Soundcloud.

(prod. WILLIE G)
(a message to tina belcher part 2)
Notes:
OG Filename: teeth full interlude.wav
Features harmonic, pitched up X vocals meant to resemble a female's voice over a WILLIE G beat sampling "no, the moon" by Teen Suicide. X confirmed this song to be part two to "a message to tina belcher", which is evident in the spoken monlogue in the second half of the track where he speaks on his breakup and his pessimistic thoughts on love in general. Could be about Cinthia as there is a clear connection between her and her teeth in X's music. This appeared on multiple tracklists for "Heartbreak Hotel". Was re-released on all streaming platforms with its sample finally being cleared by Teen Suicide on June 19th 2024 after it was announced the day before.

A collaboration that samples a scene from "The Dark Knight" and uses and exclusive beat produced by MarkerHIts. One of the few pre 2017 songs that officially released for streaming. Features heavily pitched up vocals as X had supposedly asked the engineer to speed them. Lyrics are often said to be from the full version of "RUN UP ON ME" but for now that is just a rumour.

The song is about having sex with multiple girls, as well as other people’s girls. Amy Winehouse was an English singer who sung about emotional topics. This song has a depressing mood, considering he says he's self harming like Amy Winehouse did. Featured on Members Only Vol. 1 The beat was also used by Lil Peep for his song "Old Me".

The mastered version of the extremely popular collaboration between X & Ski. Showcases versatility of the two going back and forth on the track. Used the song to get out of a traffic ticket a bicyclist passes by and screams "that shits wack!" at the end,

Name:
Members Only Vol. 2

(20/04/2015) (X releases Members Only Vol. 1.)

The 22-track mixtape contains features from founders XXXTENTACION and Ski Mask the Slump God, Fukkit, Flyboy Tarantino, and more.

CHAPTER 2 (SNIPPET)

Notes: Presumably a different snippet of the same song or the exact same thing as the clip that was uploaded before this, which was uploaded April 30th 2015. The song didn't have a cover so it was his Soundcloud profile picture at the time.

Name:
⭐RIOT
(prod. Luke White)
(Chapter 10)
Notes:
OG Filename: RIOT , CHAPTER 10.wav
A more political track, this abrasive, boom bap rap is one of X's iconic songs that encapsulates his lyrical talent as well as his presence on the mic. It was released just months after the deaths of Michael Brown, among others. After Brown’s murder in particular, several riots occurred in Ferguson, Missouri. The song would eventually find a music video that was included within the video for “Look at Me!” on September 12th, 2017.

Name:
Red Light Lie (YAWNS Re-Fix)
(prod. YXXXNZ)
Notes: "Red Light Lie (YAWNS Re-Fix)" Is a supposed instrumental release of the Red Light District track "red light lie" that was released onto YXXXNZ's Soundcloud account. This version of the track is lost. Like "Red" many rumours of this upload have been made but all are fake.

Name:
✨Rare, Part 2
(prod. Lirker)
Notes:
OG Filename: Rare , Part 2.wav
First track posted that would end up on MOV2 but was the closing track to the project. Was posted to his youtube and was the last before 2017. Similar to “Riot”, X raps about murdering and committing heinous crimes as part of the street life. Noticeably, X showcases some of his Jamaican heritage by using Patois twice in the hook of the track. The cover was a photo x took himself. Samples "Wagons" by Smolke DZA & Joey Bada$$.

Name:
Chapter 1
(prod. Fifty Grand & Kellbender)
Notes:
OG Filename: chapter 1.mp3
Prequel to "Chapter 2", one of X's darkest songs, joining his horrorcore category of tracks. With a monotonous flow and aggressively pessimistic lyrics, x showcases his violent side, with murder, pain and drugs being a main topic in this song. The instrumental samples Rob Zombies "Call Of The Zombie" the intro to his album "Hillbilly Deluxe". Unknown where the crying baby comes from. The cover was an actual black square and not a pic he took. Released on X's SoundCloud but he later deleted it and Fifty Grand decided to reupload it with a better mix and was considered for MOV2.

Name:
🏆 Chapter 2
(prod. ???)
Notes: One of the biggest grails in the community. Although it's theorized to be darker in content than its predecessor, the lead (who heard this song while it was available) is not 100% reliable. Yung Gook, a friend of X's at the time, asked X (in a now deleted tweet) why he took the song down a day after its upload, saying it was "riding". However, the SoundCloud Panel shows X deleted the song on May 30th, along with "Chapter 1", so it's possible he privated the song after a day, eventually deleting it on the 30th.

“Chapter 1 (outro)” is a remastered version of XXXTENTACION’s “Chapter 1”. The song was uploaded on XXXTENTACION’s SoundCloud back in May 24th, 2015 and later switched to private. The description of the song on SoundCloud was "</3 , sneak peek, members only vol.2 coming , “very” soon." Although it is unsure, fans have speculated that the song was scrapped from one of Members Only’s collaborative albums, Members Only Vol. 2. The track was considered “lost” until it was leaked on August 20th, 2019 through a file-sharing website. The private link later leaked on March 21, 2020.

It is a depressing dedication to his ex, Cinthia, (X's W.R.A.C.), who cried upon first hearing it. X sent the lyrics to the song to some people before releasing it. Both "Wing Ridden Angel" and "W.R.A.C." are tattooed on X’s right arm and phalanges of his right hand, respectively. Producer Kellbender has also gone on record saying he could and would recite this song word-for-word for a million dollars. Samples the song "Poachers Pride" by Nicole Dollanganger

Netherrack is a block from Minecraft that burns forever once it’s lit and that can be exclusively found in the Nether, an alternative dimension in the game that is supposed to resemble Hell. Ironically, Netherrack is one of the fastest blocks that the player can break, opposing to the alternative title of the track “I JUST WONT BREAK”. The cover art of the snippet is the texture file for Netherrack.

Was deleted when clearing it for streaming and slightly shortened and the reupload to x's account uses the full pic of kilos cut for some reason. Samples "BATTLE CRY" by Optic Core (HELLION) & Fifty Grand and "Meeting and vesting of Bishop: Cherubic Hymn" by Novospassky Monastery Choir.

Later used as the intro to MOV4 with Corey (x's brother) to talk over about memories with x, recorded october 2018.

Denzel and X's first diss towards SpaceGhostPurrp. The song was written in reaction to Purrp's series of disrespectful tweets about A$AP Yams’s death. Denzel explained in a January 2018 interview with Montreality that the song was written after a soccer game on the first day that he actually got to know X and Ski. Two days later, Denzel and x released the follow-up song “Purrposely”. Purrp retaliated with “TRAINING DAY END OF DENZEL CURRY” before Denzel said in a tweet that he had “won” and that he wouldn’t release any more diss songs towards Purrp. X released his third diss to Purrp, “#ImSippinTeainYoHood,” a month later. Ski's verse was recorded before he knew the song was a diss which is why he doesnt diss purrp.

“RIP Roach” (also referred to as “R.I.P ROACH “EAST SIDE SOULJA”) was released on May 12, 2016 by Ski Mask the Slump God, featuring his long-time companion, XXXTENTACION. The track was rereleased within X’s debut commercial mixtape, Revenge, under his name, featuring Ski. The track fits in a series of very popular tracks with heavy 808s and a set of hats, including Ski’s “Take A Step Back” and X’s “Yung Bratz”. There is a long story behind the track’s title. “Roach” was a nickname of one of X’s very close friends who was murdered in 2016. Song sampled The Fog by SpaceGhostPurrp. The track was also placed on the "Revenge" comiplation.

He made a tracklist for the project that was posted and had many features that most features he didn't have at the time, Due to X's going to jail for Alleged Sexual Assault the police confisgated his belongings along with him being in jail which had delayed the project completely. When X got out of jail, in a March 2017 interview, X stated he was working on Bad Vibes Forever alongside 17. 17 was released as his debut album in August 2017, but Bad Vibes Forever was nowhere to be found. It was later revealed that X had scrapped Bad Vibes Forever. The only songs we have heard are, Might Off Myself For 2017, Yung Bratz, and DEATHWISH

(30/06/2017) (Members Only Vol. 3 is released)
(25/08/2017) (17 is released exclusively to streaming)
Streaming: This album was first announced on X’s Periscope when he was released on probation in March. He also addressed it in his interview with 103.5 The Beat. On July 30th, X announced the release date on his Instagram Story, which was August 25th. X tweeted that “Fuck Love” will be the only song off 17 that’ll be posted on SoundCloud. It was removed after X’s Soundcloud was hacked but later re-uploaded. This song held the record for most streams of any song on the SoundCloud platform with over 275 million plays until BTS took the record.

This song was performed over “im closing my eyes,” a potsu-produced song that samples the lyrics from Shiloh Dynasty’s “I Knew You So Well.” Track 2 on "17".

X faces charges of domestic abuse and false imprisonment but has not been convicted of any of the alleged charges. His trial was set for October 5th and then has been postponed. Track 11 on "17". Would later be remixed by Kid Cudi for his album INSANO in 2024.

Name:
⭐ Noah Cyrus - Again
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Labrinth)
Notes: “Again” received considerable controversy, considering the involvement of X. Despite this, “Again” makes for a smooth and dream-like pop duet, in which X becomes the latest in the hip-hop trend of recording a song entirely sung.Noah Cryus said this when asked about the song "I wrote ‘Again’ after my past relationship and I had, we kind of got into a little argument I just talked about, ‘We need to take a break,’ and I was like, ‘A break?,’ And it was kind of about that. You really do take each other for granted and then the second somebody says, ‘Well, let’s take a break, let’s break up,’ it’s, ‘No, no, no, no, no,’ you know what I mean? It’s very like you want that person back immediately, you miss them from the second those words come out of their mouth."

Streaming: Released on March 16, 2018, ? is XXXTENTACION’s second studio album. On January 28, the cover art was revealed on X’s Instagram story with the caption, “coming soon.” X went to his Instagram story again on January 28th to explain what the album will sound like, On January 31, a picture surfaced on John Cunningham’s Twitter, producer of “Ayala (Outro)”, off of X’s debut album, 17. The picture showed that “?” had been privately uploaded to Soundcloud. On March 11, X confirmed that the album would be released on March 16th. The following day, X posted the tracklist on his Instagram story.

Was frequently teased in the intro of X’s gaming videos on his YouTube channel prior to its release. Its finished music video was released 10 days after X's death. It broke Spotify’s record for the most streams in a day. On June 19th, 2018, a day after X was shot and killed, the song was played 10.4 million times on the streaming platform. The previous record was held by Taylor Swift’s “Look What You Made Me Do” which garnered 10.1 million streams in a day. The instrumental, voice memo, and voice memo detailing his plans for the music video were all released on "? (Deluxe)" on September 6th, 2019.

Streaming: SKINS is XXXTENTACION’s third studio album and his first full-length posthumous release. It is also a follow-up to his second studio album, ?, which was released almost eight months prior. The ten-track album boasts a sole vocal feature from Kanye West.

Name:
✨ hearteater
(prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
Notes: The final song to be released in X's lifetime. Deleted shortly after being uploaded. On March 18th, 2018, two days after the release of "?", X asked his fans to get a Miami charity’s Instagram page to 30,000 followers. In return, he promised to release a song that did not make the album’s final tracklist. The song was eventually released through Brain Bakery Mag’s SoundCloud page. In an August 2019 interview with Billboard, X’s manager, Solomon Sobande, revealed that the song would be included on X’s final studio album, "Bad Vibes Forever". On October 18th, 2019, X’s estate released a teaser announcing that Geneva Ayala would appear in the song’s music video, in which she is depicted eating a heart. The song was officially remastered and released on October 22th, 2019, and the music video premiered on the 25th.

Name:
Bhad Bhabie - Trust Me
(feat. Ty Dolla $Ign) (prod. Bobby Kritical)
Notes:
OG Filename: Trust Me - Main (DroMix 8) (MASTERED MM).wav
“Trust Me” marks a turning point in Bhad Bhabie’s young and controversial rap career. For the first time, she isn’t going all out in dissing those that oppose her works. She’s transitioning from being a rebellious teenage girl of old and turning into a mature woman, while still keeping her ignorant and carefree persona intact. This song is also one of Bhad Bhabie’s most melodic, in an emotional sing rap style she hasn’t quite touched since releasing “Mama Don’t Worry.” This song was originally supposed to feature X, but in an interview with No Jumper, Bhad Bhabie revealed that he had chose to have his verse removed due to his conflicts with Lil Yachty at the time, who is featured on the mixtape.

Era: Jahseh Onfroy (a.k.a. XXXTENTACION) passes away June 18th, 2018 due to a robbery gone wrong.

The original version and accidental upload of Trippie Redd's "GHOST BUSTERS" featuring X and Ski Mask to SoundCloud and WorldStarHipHop's YouTube channel. It would eventually be replaced/deleted a few hours later to include a verse from Quavo.

Notes:
OG Filename: Trippie Redd Ft. Quavo, Ski Mask & XXXTentaction - Ghostbusters.mp3
The "finished" version of Trippie Redd's "GHOST BUSTERS" that features Ski Mask, Quavo, and X in his first posthumous appearance, three days after his death. Trippie previewed this song on March 1st, but X's management forced him to take down the snippet. This version replaced the previous version only a few hours after its accidental upload to Trippie's SoundCloud and WorldStarHipHop's YouTube channel to include Quavo's feature. It is unknown when Quavo recorded his verse, but considering the lyrical content of Ski Mask's verse, it can be assumed Quavo's was also recorded after X's death. This song would end up being reworked/remixed for Trippie's fourth studio album "Trip At Knight", renamed to "Danny Phantom" with Ski Mask and Quavo's verses removed.

This song was meant to release with an X feature as X had promised Skye a feature when he was alive but due to his passing he never got to fufill this promise. The song went through many demos which used mutiple verses from different X songs. Ultimately the X feature would be scrapped and the song would release without X. Skye would later get an X feature on another song titled "VOICES" which would release the next year.

Name:

(with Lil Peep) (prod. John Cunningham, Blue Cheeze & Mike WILL Made It)
Notes:
OG Filename: just in case2.mp3
X's remix of Lil Peep and iLoveMakonnen's collaboration "Sunlight On Your Skin", recorded prior to X's passing but after Peep's. Fish Narc, a friend of Peep, expressed his frustration with the song's existence, saying Peep didn't support X for his abuse allegations and Peep's label just wanted to profit. Smokesaac eventually confirmed the song's existence and imminent release on Instagram. Peep's own mother confirmed that it wasn't her choice that X ended up on the song, it was iLoveMakonnen's. The voice memo detailing X's contributions to the song dated January 18th, 2018 leaked on November 7th, 2022 by Jacob. A remix of the X and Peep version of the song by Travis Barker was released on July 11th, 2019.

Name:

Notes: X & Lil Wayne's first collab. Acts as the intro track to Lil Wayne's highly anticipated "The Carter V". X originally recorded the chorus in February 2018 with Florida native producer, Z3N, for a completely different song that is currently unknown. Z3N eventually met with artist LunchMoney Lewis who passed the vocals to the eventual producer of "Don't Cry", Ben Billions. Two music videos were shot, one with a stand-in double for X which was ultimately scrapped, with the final version of the video releasing on January 23rd, 2019: X’s 21st birthday.

Rio would be removed from the song along with the instrumental being reworked. Rio himself would express his disappointment with how the song ultimately turned out, tweeting "Original > Fake". Has a music video that is almost at 400 million views.

Name:

(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: 05 LP V2 - Bad (EQ3-Brighter) (Mix 05 3UM01) (MASTERED DK).wav
Track 5 on "SKINS", X's first posthumous album. Teased by Cleo on November 7th, 2018 before releasing two days later as the lead single for the project. X himself teased the song's chorus on Instagram before posting some lyrics on his Tumblr. A voice memo from May 18th confirms either the song's recording or intial conception. A contest to animate the music video was held, with the winner's final product premiering on December 15th, 2018, with the video now sitting at close to 200 million views.

Name:
Introduction
(prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
(Skins (Intro))
Notes:
OG Filename: 01 LP V2 - Skins (Intro) (edit 01) (MASTERED DK).wav
SKINS opens with an “Introduction” in the same fashion as XXXTENTACION’s previous albums. However, this one is unlike the introductions on those albums, as it does not include X’s voice whatsoever and is not a set of instructions; it is instead a robotic voice asking the listener a series of introspective questions. The only similarity to the previous intros is the statement that the listener is “within his mind.”

Name:
⭐ Guardian angel
(prod. potsu & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: 02 LP V2 - Guardian Angel (Choir) (Mix 06 UM) (MASTERED DK).wav
The second track on XXXTENTACION’s first posthumous album finds the late rapper discussing heartbreak and the toll that it took on him. His troublesome upbringing resulted in a vicious cycle of pain and anger, but he ultimately apologizes for harming those around him. The instrumental contains reversed samples of “Jocelyn Flores,” the second track on X’s highly successful 2017 debut 17. Jocelyn was a friend of X who committed suicide at a Florida hotel in May 2017. This track can be viewed as a spiritual successor of sorts, since both songs share thematic and sonic similarities. Additionally, in a video of X playing League of Legends, he briefly refers to Jocelyn as his “guardian angel.”

Name:
⭐ Train food
(prod. John Cunningham & XXXTENTACION)
Notes:
OG Filename: 03 LP V2 - Train Food (V3'4UM) (MASTERED DK).wav
On “Train food,” XXXTENTACION discusses death’s inescapability. The song’s minimalistic and eerie production features a jarring piano accompanied by the sounds of a train and thunder. X details a hypothetical first-person narrative about an encounter with the personification of Death. The narrator takes a different route than usual and is kidnapped by Death. They wake up tied to train tracks and deliver an emotional monologue, reflecting on their life and imagining what could have been in their future. The individual eventually wonders if they are going to heaven or hell but are ultimately killed before an answer is revealed. Based on its lyrical content, “Train food” appears to represent the opposing viewpoint of X’s infamous XXL Freshmen Cypher. In that verse, X raps from the perspective of the devil, detailing a murderous plot that involves stalking and kidnapping. Many of the lyrics in that verse directly correspond to lyrics in this song. This track is also both sonically and thematically reminiscent of 2016’s “I spoke to the devil in Miami, he said everything would be fine.”

Name:

(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: 04 LP V2 - Whoa (EQ2) (KH New Mix v5) (MASTERED DK).wav
Track 4 on "SKINS". X planned for Sia to feature on this song. After X passed, their team reached out Sia but she turned it down. There exists a remix that features Juice WRLD that was recorded prior to Juice's passing but after X's. John Cunningham confirmed Juice was "supposed to get on whoa" and DJ Scheme confirmed Juice played it for him personally, but Juice didn't record his verse until after SKINS came out, making it a remix. Matt Ox (who appeared on "?'s" "$$$") and Lil Uzi Vert were once recorded in the studio singing and dancing to a beat with the same sample as "whoa".

On “STARING AT THE SKY,” X reflects on the pain he has endured and how he aimlessly approached life at times. X previewed this song on May 19th, 2018, just under a month before his death. The second-shortest song on the album next to its interlude. feature a drop very similar to the track "Pain = BESTFRIEND".

Notes:

Track 7 on "SKINS", featuring Kanye West and blink-182's Travis Barker (who appeared on "?'s" "Pain = BESTFRIEND") on drums. X previewed this song on June 14th, 2018, four days before his death. John Cunningham confirmed that X had wanted to collaborate with Kanye right up to the day he died, while also confirming artist Scarlxrd was never intended for the song despite the rumors at the time. The song was reworked multiple times as most Kanye West tracks are, with multiple features and references being made.

Name:
difference (interlude)
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: 08 LP V2 - Difference (no master'01) (MASTERED DK).wav
On “difference (interlude),” X is pleading to a woman in his life, discussing the things he wants to eventually provide for her. However, their relationship has limitations because of their distance from each other. X could be referring to Jenesis Sanchez, his long-time girlfriend and the mother of his child, Gekyume. Their relationship was kept a secret, and Jenesis was first revealed to the world only a few days before SKINS‘ release. The minimalistic guitar chord is sonically reminiscent of “love yourself (interlude),” and this track may have been recorded during the same studio session.  A voice memo of The Weeknd covering this song leaked on September 27th, 2023.

Name:
I don't let go
(prod. Nils, John Cunningham & CuBeatz)
Notes:
OG Filename: 09 LP V2 - I Don't Let Go (KH Final Mix) (MASTERED DK).wav
On “I don’t let go,” XXXTENTACION tells the listener that he does not let go of himself because he knows he has others to live for. The uptempo track makes several references to women and sexual endeavors, and X utilizes several flows over the song’s two verses. Uses the same loop as Rich the Kid's "Bring It Back" and was further utilized in Jaden Smith's "Better Things". Allegedly set to initially feature Future.

Name:
⭐ what are you so afraid of
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: 10 LP V2 - What Are You So Afraid Of (RS Mix01) (MASTERED DK).wav
“what are you so afraid of” is the final track from XXXTENTACION’s first posthumous album, SKINS. X questions the listener’s love choices and fears repeatedly as he may have believed love is a waste of time. X had dealt with heartbreak before with his ex-girlfriend, Geneva Ayala, who he started dating in 2014. After two years and multiple domestic violence claims from her, the couple broke up, causing X to feel broken. He wrote the song “Ayala (Outro)” about her on 17 expressing how he was hurt by her and regretted being with her. “before I close my eyes” has a similar guitar tone.

The audio clip is taken from an extensive 2016 Periscope live session, and the instrumental in the background is produced by Killstation. On March 15, 2019, X’s estate launched a timer on X’s official merchandise website, which counted down to March 22.
