Name: Bad Vibes Forever (2019)
Notes:
(06/09/2019) (? (Deluxe) is released)
(06/12/2019) (Bad Vibes Forever is released)
Streaming: Bad Vibes Forever is XXXTENTACION’s fourth and final studio album. It’s also his second album to be released posthumously, following SKINS exactly a year prior. “Bad Vibes Forever” is a multi-faceted company created by X that encompasses a record label, fashion brand, and more. The brand has been closely associated with him since the inception of his career. In a March 2018 “vlog,” X explained exactly what “Bad Vibes Forever” means to him.

Name:
✨ HEARTEATER
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: The official release & update of the 2018 single, the last song released before his death. The track was repurposed to be the second single for Bad Vibes Forever, eventually appearing as track 21. On October 18th, 2019, X’s estate released a teaser announcing that Geneva Ayala would appear in the song’s music video, in which she is depicted eating X's heart. The video begins with a voice memo from April 12th, 2018, in which X explains his concept for the song’s music video & why he wanted Ayala to be in it. The music video, recorded without X's personal involvement but under his limited guidance, was released three days after the song, on October 25th, 2019. According to John Cunningham, X wanted to rework the song from its initial March version, saying it "didn't sound as good as it should". The updates to the mix & drums stem from his vision for the song.

Name:
Skye - VOICES
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Kid Joki)
Notes:
OG Filename: Skye - VOICES ft. XXXTentacion.wav
X's posthumous feature on Daniel Skye's song, the first official collaboration between both artists. Snippets of the song appeared months before its release, and the song was leaked a couple hours before its scheduled release date. X’s verse comes from his scrapped 2018 “Yes Indeed” freestyle, his remix of the hit Drake and Lil Baby collab. This verse was initially given to Kanye West and appeared on his song “The Storm”, but both the song and the album it was supposed to appear on were scrapped, allowing for the vocals to be repurposed on Skye's track. The chorus draws inspiration from blink-182’s “I Miss You”, a band that would ultimately feature on the final track of X's final album, Bad Vibes Forever. X and Daniel Skye had known each other prior to his death, and according to people close to them, X promised Skye a feature, a promise he unfortunately could not fulfill. This song ensures that promise was kept. A demo version of the song leaked, utilizing X's uncut verse, containing completely different Skye vocals (apart from the chorus), and an overall different instrumentation. A music video for the finalized released version premiered on the song's release date.

Name:
⭐ bad vibes forever
(feat. Trippie Redd & PnB Rock) [prod. John Cunningham]
Notes: The third single for Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Trippie Redd & PnB Rock. The song released two weeks before the album, eventually appearing as track 4. The original solo demo was recorded sometime in 2017 at the same studio X created his debut album, 17, at. The vision for the instrumentation was discussed, but not finalized until after his death. X allegedly stated he wanted live drums on the song, which were added (alongside the features) posthumously. The song was also added to Trippie Redd's fourth mixtape, A Love Letter To You 4, with both the single & mixtape releasing on the same day, the song being added to it four days later. For unknown reasons, Trippie would remove the song from his project (perhaps issues with it also appearing on X's posthumous one). A music video for the song premiered on December 5th, 2019.

Name:
Trippie Redd - All For Me
(feat. Smokepurrp) (prod. Bans & Stunner Samples)
Notes:
OG Filename: 11. All For Me Ft. Smokepurpp (DIRTY).wav
Collaboration track between Ohio's Trippie Redd and Florida's Smokepurrp with X on writing credits bedause of the featuring of an X outro at the end of the track. Track 11 off Trippie Redd's "A Love Letter To You 4". This clip was taken from X’s first vlog on YouTube, titled My Heart, and Yours: In the clip, X discusses not wanting to search for love anymore after people in the past have done him wrong. Trippie uses this to reiterate his own feelings towards love, which are found elsewhere on the album, such as “Who Needs Love.”

Name:
introduction
(prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 1 on Bad Vibes Forever. Similar to his previous studio albums, X speaks, though this time over an instrumental produced by John Cunningham. John chose an excerpt of a voice memo to play over it. According to Cleo (X's mother), the conversation sampled was recorded in mid-January 2018. John played the instrumental for X, who loved it & wanted it to become a full song. He wrote for it, but never recorded for it.

Name:
⭐ Ex Bitch
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 2 on Bad Vibes Forever. It can be assumed the song is in reference to his ex-girlfriend, Geneva Ayala. John Cunningham posted a picture of this song's recording process, saying it was his favorite song they made together. Started recording on January 31st, 2018.

Name:
⭐ UGLY
(prod. XXXTENTACION & Grouper)
Notes: Track 3 on Bad Vibes Forever. This is the finished version of the iconic & grailed 2017 snippet, previewed by X himself on Snapchat. That version had a longer intro, different mixing, & an open verse. According to John Cunningham, this song was left completely untouched as X had created the entire track himself on Audacity. This implies that sometime after X previewed the song on Snapchat, he continued to work on & finish it, exporting the version heard on the 2019 posthumous album. He sampled Grouper's "Invisible" after hearing it for the first time. A demo of the track featuring Members Only's ikabodVEINS that was developed a month after X's death leaked on January 18th, 2021 & is largely regarded as the worst leak in the community of all time.

Name:
School Shooters
(feat. Lil Wayne) [prod. John Cunningham]
Notes: Track 5 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Lil Wayne. This song this marks the duo's third collaboration, following Wayne’s September 2018 Tha Carter V opening track, “Don’t Cry”, and the December 2018 Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse soundtrack song, “Scared of the Dark”. This song was recorded, without Wayne, alongside "Hope" (which would later release on ?) after the February 2018 shooting at Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida. Due to the violent nature of X's lyrics, he ultimately ended up choosing "Hope" to finish & release as a single for his sophomore album. A music video for this song was released on April 10th, 2020, and an exclusive remix by Kemba (who later features on "Daemons" on this album) was released to his YouTube channel on February 18th, 2020, with different mixing & added (albeit weird) Wayne adlibs.

Name:
I Changed Her Life
(feat. Rick Ross) (prod. John Cunningham)
(Changed her fucking life)
Notes: Track 6 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Rick Ross. This is the duo's first & sole collaboration. Ross was added to the original, unfinished demo sometime after X’s death. According to YouTuber Lael Hansen, who spoke to X multiple times, he wanted Ross to feature on the track.

Name:
⭐ Triumph
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 7 on Bad Vibes Forever. This song could be about X's baby mama, Jenesis Sanchez. X & Jen met through a mutual friend at a party. They started dating in January 2018, but knew each other some years prior. In February 2018, Jen moved from her home in Tampa to Parkland in order to be closer to X. Their son, Gekyume, was born in January 2019, seven months after X's death. In a Reddit AMA, Cleopatra Bernard (X's mother), stated this was his favorite song he had made. In a Discord AMA with John Cunningham, I (Mockingbird) asked if the song was finished prior to X's death, to which John replied "yes."

Name:
⭐ LIMBO
(feat. Killstation) (prod. Killstation & JUDGE)
(Window)
Notes: Track 8 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Members Only's Killstation. This is the sixth, second posthumous & potentially final collaboration between the two. The song was originally a solo Killstation track slated to appear on XXXTENTACION Presents: Members Only, Vol. 4, as confirmed in an ImDontai YouTube reaction video from January 24th, 2019. Craig Xen accidentally uploaded the solo version instead of "Love Hard, Fall Fast". It is unclear where X's vocals originate, though it has been theorized they were from the posthumous Lil Peep collab, "Falling Down". This has never been proven.

Name:
before i realize
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 9 on Bad Vibes Forever. A slow acoustic track that, while not directly stated who it's in reference to, seems to be about Geneva Ayala, X's ex-girlfriend. When snippets of the album leaked before its release, this was thought to be fourth installment of the beloved Alone trilogy. Was recorded on January 16th, 2018 (as confirmed by John Cunningham himself).

Name:
Ecstasy
(feat. Noah Cyrus) (prod. XXYYXX)
Notes: Track 10 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Noah Cyrus. This marks the second collaboration between both artists, following their 2017 mega-hit, "Again". This is partially an official release of the 2014 track of the same name, with the original sample's vocals by Anneka re-recorded by Cyrus because of sample clearance issues. Some pitched vocals from Anneka remain in this posthumous mix. This is one-of-two tracks originally recorded in 2014 that appear on the album, the other being "Daemons".

Name:
🗑️ Kill My Vibe
(feat. Tom G) (prod. Jasper Sheff & John Cunningham)
(DIE)
Notes: Track 11 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Tom G. This track marks the first & only collaboration between both artists. An alternative demo version featuring Trippie Redd (entitled "DIE") in place of Tom G leaked on January 16th, 2021, with an open verse allegedly meant for Playboi Carti, who (unsurprisingly) never recorded for the track. It's unclear why the estate chose Tom G for the album, who has never publicly contacted or associated with X in any way. This, along with "Hot Gyal", are two of the most criticized posthumous songs released by X's estate.

Name:
🗑️ Hot Gyal
(feat. Mavado & Tory Lanez) (prod. Mally Mall & JonFX)
(In Heaven, Forever, In N Out, Sayonara)
Notes: Track 12 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Tory Lanez and Mavado. A not-so-well rework of a 2017 feature X recorded as a remix of Vybz Kartel's "In Heaven", which eventually turned into 2018's "Forever". Due to clearance issues, neither songs released, though X disapproved of how his vocals were mixed on "Forever". However, John Cunningham confirmed X wanted the autotune on his vocals heard in "Forever" & "Hot Gyal". A version featuring (posthumously-recorded) vocals from Rihanna was leaked on January 30th, 2021, though it's unclear why she was replaced with Tory for the final version. Allegedly, there were problems with her behind-the-scenes. A poor music video was released on December 11th, 2019. One of the most criticized posthumous decisions his estate has made (along with the entire project it appears on, unfortunately).

Name:
THE ONLY TIME I FEEL ALIVE
(feat. Craig Xen) (prod. Jasper Sheff & John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 13 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Craig Xen. An original demo version of this track, featuring a different Craig verse, leaked on June 15th, 2020. It's unclear which verse, if either, were recorded prior to X's death, and why the original verse was scrapped for the song's release. Features an acoustic outro of the song's hook that's possibly a voice memo.

Name:
the interlude that never ends
(prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 14 on Bad Vibes Forever. This song was recorded in 2017, first previewed by X on The Revenge Tour. X never wanted to release this track and when asked about it, spoke negatively on it. On r/XXXTENTACION, John Cunningham said that X recorded two songs with the title “The Interlude That Never Ends” (the other eventually being renamed to “numb the pain”, appearing as track 24 on Bad Vibes Forever), verifying a part of the lyrics for both tracks in the process. The original recording & version of this song is lost, possibly deleted by X himself, leaving this version more-than-likely a remake of the snippet. The original is considered a grail within the community. Some believe the version on BVF V1 to be the elusive 2017 snippet, though this has never been confirmed, only implied due to every song in that folder being in their original, largely untouched version. Why that version has such unique production and was changed for the album's release is unknown.

Name:
✨ Daemons
(feat. Joey Bada$$ & Kemba) (prod. Chanslin)
(Angels 2 Demons)
Notes: Track 15 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Joey Bada$$ & Kemba. The track marks the third collaboration between X & Joey, though the first with Kemba. X heard & enjoyed Kemba's album before his death & Kemba would eventually sign to John's imprint at Republic Records, explaining his appearance on the remix. X’s (censored) verse & the song's instrumental are from an old feature with J $oul, 2014's "Angels 2 Demons", a remix of “Who The Fuck is God”, a song written about X's uncle who committed suicide. The instrumental comes from a free 2013 YouTube video entitled “Kanye West type beat.” This is the second track that was originally recorded in 2014 that appears on the album, the other being "Ecstasy" with Noah Cyrus. It's unknown why the mention of "God" is censored in X's verse, but it was most likely to avoid controversy.

Name:
✨ ATTENTION!
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 16 on Bad Vibes Forever. “ATTENTION!” is a braggadocious track that shows XXXTENTACION demanding everyone to pay attention to him, as the chorus suggests. On the song, X brags about his cash and warns you not to mess with him. John Cunningham confirmed this song to have been recorded on April 21st, 2018, after playing it to X on a keyboard.

Name:
✨ Eat It Up
(prod. RONNY J)
Notes: Track 17 on Bad Vibes Forever, potentially acting as the final collaboration between X & producer RONNY J. The song is possibly a leftover from X’s December 2017 EP, A GHETTO CHRISTMAS CAROL!, which was primarily produced by RONNY J. When that project released, RONNY J posted to his Instagram with this song's title as the caption. However, album credits list the song as being recorded at XXX Studios, which wasn't built until 2018. When the song was recorded is currently unknown. Ronny previewed the song on his Instagram on May 26th, 2019. One-of-two RONNY J-produced songs on the album, the other being the reworked "NorthStar - Remix".

Name:
Voss
(feat. Sauce Walka & Carnage) (prod. Gordo)
Notes: Track 18 on Bad Vibes Forever, acting as the official release of the sole X & Sauce Walka 2018 collaboration. X handles the track’s chorus while Walka provides a lengthy verse. “Voss” was originally the seventh track on Walka’s August 2018 mixtape, Drip God, but the song was removed from the tape within days of its release for unknown reasons. A video of X recording the chorus (and a scrapped verse) confirms the song was recorded on November 21st, 2017 alongside "Middle Child" with PnB Rock.

Name:
⭐ wanna grow old (i won't let go)
(feat. Jimmy Levy) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Track 20 on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring Jimmy Levy. The track serves as the first & sole collaboration between X & Levy. X only has a chorus reminiscent of his 2018 track, “I don’t let go”. John Cunningham confirmed in an AMA that X's chorus (and the initial instrumentation) were recorded in late May 2018, making this one of the last songs X recorded before his murder in June 2018.

Name:
🗑️ NorthStar - Remix
(feat. Joyner Lucas) (prod. Mally Mall & RONNY J)
(Shining Like the Northstar)
Notes: Track 22 on Bad Vibes Forever & Joyner Lucas's official remix of X's 2018 track, "Shining Like the Northstar". This is their sole collaboration. The two DM'd back-and-forth & likely planned to collaborate prior to X's death, though it's unclear if this would have been the product of that collaboration. The track's posthumous rework originally featured Tyga, with his version leaking on April 28th, 2020. It’s unclear why he was cut from the final version. One-of-two RONNY J-produced songs on the album, the other being "Eat It Up".

Name:
🗑️ CHASE / glass shards
(feat. ikabodVEINS) (prod. XXXTENTACION)
Notes: Track 23 on Bad Vibes Forever. This song marks the only collaboration between X & Members Only's ikabodVEINS, who tattooed X a few times. This song was one of the last X previewed while he was alive, with him teasing his verse on Instagram live in May 22nd, 2018. This is also the last piece of music X worked on before he died, making changes to the instrumental (mainly the drum beat-switch) mere hours before his death on June 18th, 2018. The opening of the track shares the same sample as his 2014 tracks, "0C3AN" & "INUYASHA". This shows how X wanted to revisit his old sound while putting a more modern spin on it (according to frequent producer prxz), seen in how he produced this particular song himself. According to X's mixing engineer, Koen Heldens, ikabod was meant to be featured on the track. A demo version of the song with a slightly different use of the intro sample was group-bought & leaked on February 2nd, 2020. It's unknown when that version was made. A forgettable and regrettable music video was released on October 15th, 2020.

Name:
⭐ numb the pain
(prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
(Hate Being Alone)
Notes: The 24th & penultimate track on Bad Vibes Forever. Recorded on January 7th, 2018, confirmed as X previewed the song as a live acoustic performance in a now-deleted Instagram post. In the video, X sings over the guitar played by John Cunningham. The track was also meant to be on 2018's ? at some point, as seen in the Robbie Box folders, but under a different name ("hate being alone"). According to John, they recorded this song multiple times in the studio, but never liked how it sounded, so had this song released while X was alive, it likely would have sounded just as it does on the posthumous album, which isn't hard to believe considering the interludes on ? and 17.

Name:
IT'S ALL FADING TO BLACK
(feat. blink-182) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: The 25th & final track on Bad Vibes Forever, featuring the popular band blink-182. The woman X sings about could be ex-girlfriend Geneva Ayala. It is the sole collaboration between X & blink-182, though X had collaborated with their drummer, Travis Barker, multiple times while he was alive, most notably on ?'s "pain = BESTFRIEND". A solo demo version of the track featuring a short, rough mumble verse from X leaked on January 15th, 2020, only a month after the posthumous mix released. According to John Cunningham, X "screamed" in excitement over the song & wanted blink-182 to feature on it. It was also the most challenging song on the album to produce.

Name: LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM
Notes:
(06/12/2019) (Bad Vibes Forever is released)
(10/06/2023) (LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM is released)
Streaming: Released as a double album, LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM is XXXTENTACION’s third posthumous album following December 2019’s Bad Vibes Forever. This compilation of songs released throughout X’s career serves as a soundtrack to Look At Me: XXXTENTACION, a documentary about the life of XXXTENTACION that was released on May 26, 2022.

Name:
JAMN - Angels 2 Demons (OG REMIX)
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. ChansliN)
(Daemons)
Notes:
OG Filename: ANGELS2.mp3
Rapper J $oul's (now JAMN) official remix of his only known collaboration with X. JAMN has gone on record saying he personally disliked his original verse on the song, which is why he re-recorded a completely new one after the original version leaked (and the Joey Bada$$ and Kemba remix released on Bad Vibes Forever). X's vocals were recorded sometime in 2014, with JAMN updating his verse on the song sometime after X's death. The track would ultimately be deleted sometime in either late 2023 or early 2024.

Name:
Lil Wayne - Get Outta My Head
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Natra Average & prxz)
(THE BOY WITH THE BLACK EYES)
Notes: Lil Wayne's remix and rework of X's 2017 track "THE BOY WITH THE BLACK EYES". The song, now titled "Get Outta My Head", appears as track 18 on Wayne's thirteenth studio album, Funeral. X's original chorus remains mostly identical, with the notable inclusion of new Wayne vocals. This remix marks the fourth (posthumous) collaboration between both artists, following 2018's "Don't Cry" and "Scared of the Dark", and 2019's "School Shooters", respectively. Was poorly received and criticized by fans and even the original producers, prxz and NatraAverage.

Name:
Kemba - School Shooters (Remix)
(feat. XXXTENTACION & Lil Wayne) (prod. Jasper Sheff & John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: X joint.mp3
An exclusive remixed version of "School Shooters" featuring Lil Wayne that was recorded after its inclusion on Bad Vibes Forever. This remix includes two new verses from Kemba and a few new adlibs from Lil Wayne. Moreover, Wayne's verse is mixed cleaner and in an overall higher quality than it was on the posthumous album. The remix was released on February 18th, 2020 to Kemba’s YouTube channel and SoundCloud, as a thank you to the r/XXXTENTACION community for the love they showed on his feature.

Name:
Nyora Spouse - NightmareZ
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. WILLIE G)
Notes:
OG Filename: 1. NightmareZ ft. xxx.mp3
Official release of the 2015 collaboration onto streaming services by Nyora himself. X's contributions to the track are left uncredited, leaving the song's release quite unknown by the general public. It contains no audible differences from its original SoundCloud release.

Name:
Riot
(prod. Luke White)
Notes: The semi-official release of the 2015 fan-favorite track, as the Klansman outro excerpt was removed from the song for streaming. “Riot” was released onto streaming platforms on June 1st, 2020, notably amidst nationwide protests of George Floyd’s unjust and unlawful murder. One of the major aspects of these protests were violent riots and the looting of businesses across the country, so X’s message in this song was an inadvertant foreshadowing of the then-current state of America. The streaming version cuts the song down to 1:19, and it was released under the title “Riot” rather than the original all-caps title from SoundCloud, and features a brand new cover art instead of the simple black background that the cover art was originally.

Name:
MANIKIN
(feat. Wifisfuneral) (prod. GRIMMDoza)
Notes:
OG Filename: 01 MANIKIN (Feat. Wifisfuneral).mp3
The re-release of the 2016 ItWasntEnough EP track that was released by producer GRIMMDoza onto streaming platforms, before ultimately getting removed. A few weeks later, GRIMMDoza would also release "Jugg Walk!" onto streaming which also got removed. "MANIKIN", however, would eventually end up on streaming, re-releasing alongside the rest of ItWasntEnough EP in 2023.

Name:
Jugg Walk
(prod. GRIMMDoza)
Notes:
OG Filename: 06 Jugg Walk! (Bad Quality).wav.m4a
The 2016 track was uploaded by producer GRIMMDoza onto streaming, similar to "MANIKIN", but the track was ultimately removed, having yet to see an official re-release as of 2025.

Name:
🗑️ Vice City - NFT
(prod. Canis Major)
Notes: A NFT release of the signature 2014 track. This version of the song has an increased instrumental volume. Stephen Bliss, the artist behind the iconic artwork for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City did the NFT artwork for this release.

Name:
🗑️ #IMSIPPINTEAINYOHOOD - NFT
(prod. RONNY J)
Notes: A NFT release of the 2016 mega-hit. It has two new, different cover arts. Stephen Bliss, the artist behind the iconic artwork for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City did the NFT artwork for this release.

Name:
🗑️ News/Flock - NFT
(prod. Mixla)
Notes: A NFT release of X's first ever song from 2013. This version removes the Jamaican feature prominent in the original version that was uploaded to X's SoundCloud account. This version was simply pulled from someone's edit of the song on SoundCloud. Stephen Bliss, the artist behind the iconic artwork for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City did the NFT artwork for this release. #JusticeForJamaicanMan

Name:
​🗑️ i spoke to the devil in miami, he said everything will be fine - NFT
(prod. alasen & jakubkaminsk)
Notes: A NFT release of the 2016 masterpiece. There aren't many differences, though the cover art is a bit different from the original. Stephen Bliss, the artist behind the iconic artwork for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City, did the NFT artwork for this release.

Name:
🗑️ Let's Pretend We're Numb - NFT
(prod. KHAED)
Notes: A NFT release of the fan-favorite 2015 track. There are no differences to the song, only a new cover art. Part of the infamous NFT collection. Stephen Bliss, the artist behind the iconic artwork for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City, did the NFT artwork for this release.

Name:
Trippie Redd - DANNY PHANTOM
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Starboy, OUTTATOWN & Cashmere Cat)
(GHOST BUSTERS)
Notes: The rework and official release of X's first posthumous release, June 2018's "GHOST BUSTERS" with Trippie Redd, Quavo, and Ski Mask. This new version, retitled to "Danny Phantom", appears track 12 on Trippie's fifth studio album, Trip At Knight. The song is backed by a new “rage” style instrumental (to match the sound of the album it appears on), and doesn’t contain the original verses from both Quavo and Ski Mask. There appear to be new adlibs that suggest an earlier demo of the song, previously unheard until this version's release.

Name:
vice city
(prod. Canis Major)
Notes:
OG Filename: vice city.wav
Official release of iconic 2014 track that has no differences. Its release to all streaming platforms was announced on X's birthday in a video on his posthumous channel titled "RETURN TO MAKEOUTHILL". The song would later appear on LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM, as track 1.

Name:
Kanye West - True Love
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Ojivolta, Kanye West & John Cunningham)
Notes: The first version of the X & Kanye West collaboration, released on the Stem Player website on February 24th, 2022 but later removed. The song sees Kanye West and X singing about the complicated nature of love and their struggles to keep their relationships alive. The beat is very reminiscent of Ye’s 2010 magnum opus track, “Runaway.”

Name:
Kanye West - Selfish
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Ojivolta, Kanye West & John Cunningham)
Notes:
OG Filename: SELFISH MIKE DEAN MIX 6_06.wav
The only "official" release of the X & Kanye West collaboration, the second on his ill-fated album Donda 2. X first sang a portion of the song's chorus during an Instagram Live in November 2017, during his "Ugly" era. A ten-second, high-quality snippet of X’s studio-recorded vocals leaked online on July 11th, 2020. The song debuted on February 22nd, 2022, during the live listening event for Kanye’s scrapped eleventh studio album. It was officially released through Ye’s Stem Player two days later.

Name:
Kanye West - True Love (Stadium Version)
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Ojivolta, Kanye West & John Cunningham)
Notes: A day after the release of "True Love", the “Stadium Version” was released. This version of the song found the drums changed back to how they were heard live in Miami during the scrapped album Donda 2's listening party. After that, the initial “crushed drums” version was removed from the Stem Player, with the “Stadium Version” ultimately taking its place.

Name:
Robb Bank$ - MAY I
(feat. XXXTENTACION, Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. FaxOnly)
(May I Flex)
Notes: A posthumous collab between the Florida trio of Robb Bank$, X & Ski Mask, where X's vocals was taken from his 2017 feature on BlackBagLa and Momoh's "May I Flex". His vocals were reworked, being put over a new beat with added verses from both Robb & Ski Mask. However, the track was eventually taken down, due to Robb not clearing X's vocals with the original creators of "May I Flex", despite clearance from X's team. "MAY I" appeared as track 12 on Bank$'s FALCONIA.

Name:
⭐ True Love
(with Kanye West) (prod. Kanye West, John Cunningham, Ojivolta & MIKE DEAN)
Notes:
OG Filename: 25 LP V2 - True Love (XXXTENTACION & YE) (Sequenced DK).wav
Released as a single for both the scrapped Kanye West album, Donda 2, and the released posthumous X compilation, LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM, as the 25th and final track. Differs from both stem player versions that "released" before. Samples the song "The Basement" by Pete Rock & C.L. Smooth.

Name:
NEVER
(prod. Aesthesys)
Notes:
OG Filename: NEVER FINISHED.wav
One of X's signature songs. A very soft & mellow track with dark & depressive themes over a beautiful composition by Aesthesys. Is often cited as one of X's best songs. Part of "The Fall" EP, the fourth & final track on the 2016 EP "WILLY WONKA WAS A CHILD MURDERER", and appears on "LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM" as track two, released in 2022 to coincide with the documentary of the same name. Composer Aesthesys has stated he planned on suing X over the stealing of his work, though dropped the suit following X's death in June of 2018.

Name:
rare
(prod. Fifty Grand)
Notes:
OG Filename: rare.mp3
One of X's most infamous songs that contains violent lyrics including the brutal "I beat the bitch like Ray Rice, she won't testify if you ask her" line. Was leaked by (the goat) Bountry on December 16th, 2017 and was eventually re-released to streaming services in 2022 on the documentary album, appearing as track three. Often referred to as "Rare, Part 1". Appears as track two the "♡ ʳ ᵃ ʳ ᵉ ♡" EP.

Name:
FUXK
(feat. Ski Mask The Slump God) (prod. WILLIE G)
Notes:
OG Filename: fukk.wav
“FUXK” is a track from XXXTENTACION and Ski Mask the Slump God’s first project, Members Only, Vol.1!. It is the first song that brought serious attention to X and Ski: when asked in his No Jumper Interview when his “music started popping off on SoundCloud,” X responds “as soon as we dropped FUXK” (59:55). The song features a bass heavy instrumental with quick flows from Ski and aggressive rapping from X, albeit not the shouting style he would adopt later. “FUXK” was produced by Willie G, who also produced tracks off Members Only, Vol.1! The instrumental to each of these songs is available on Willie G’s youtube channel. The track was re-released on official streaming services on LOOK AT ME: THE ALBUM on June 10, 2022

Name:
WingRiddenAngel
(prod. Kellbender)
Notes:
OG Filename: wra.wav
Abbreviated by X himself as "W.R.A.C", "WingRiddenAngel" is one of his most recognizable and popular songs from when he was still relatively underground. It is a depressing dedication to his ex, Cinthia, (X's W.R.A.C.), who cried upon first hearing it. X sent the lyrics to the song to some people before releasing it. Both "Wing Ridden Angel" and "W.R.A.C." are tattooed on X’s right arm and phalanges of his right hand, respectively. Producer Kellbender has also gone on record saying he could and would recite this song word-for-word for a million dollars.

Name:
King Of The Dead
(prod. HELLION & Fifty Grand)
Notes: Uploaded specifically for MOV2. Another suicidal declaration by X who wants to remind the world that he’s conscious about his situation and doesn’t need any help from any one. The songs title is a nod to The Weeknd’s 2014 track “King Of The Fall”. X tweeted that song during the fall era which fit perfectly then. Was deleted when clearing it for streaming and slightly shortened and the reupload to x's account uses the full pic of kilos cut for some reason.

Name:
FAILURE IS NOT AN OPTION (Interlude)
(prod. King Yosef)
Notes:

Released onto Soundcloud at the very end of 2023 on December 31st along with the rest of Yarprak Asimov's EP. Features a new cover art which is mainly white. Old uploads of this song and many other Yaprak Asimov songs have extremely noticable botted SoundCloud plays with songs having over 100k plays and only 10 likes.

Name:

(with XXXTENTACION) (prod. Kid Cudi, Tobias Jesso Jr. & Dot Da Genius, John Cunningham, Jean Baptiste & Cathedral)
(Orlando)
Notes: Track 15 on Kid Cudi's ninth studio album, INSANO, featuring X. Is Cudi's official remix of X's "Orlando" from 17, marking the first collaboration between both artists (potentially not the last). Cudi compared the instance of X's remix of his song "Edge of the Earth" to the eventual creation of this song. He pays homage to X's melody in his first verse. Unfortunately, the track was met with mixed reception like most of X's posthumous material, solo & features.

Was released Feburary 2024 but shows as 2021, the track also had X and Trippie credited as main artists but was later removed (For the better). The song now has only X credited on Spotify as a featured artist.

Name:
Ski Mask The Slump God - Jah’s Interlude 
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. XXXTENTACION & John Cunningham)
(Hope - Freestyle)
Notes: Track 7 on Ski Mask's 2024 album 11th Dimension. It is an edited version of "Hope (Freestyle)" that released on ? (Deluxe) in August 2018. This edit contains AI-separated vocals from the voice memo, lacking the instrumental heard in the original freestyle. Ski clarified on X (Twitter) before his album released that "the X song is just a voice memo of him talking about me", perhaps to properly gauge people's expectations of the feature before hearing it.

Name:
Teeth (Interlude)
(prod. WILLIE G)
(a message to tina belcher part 2)
Notes:
OG Filename: teeth full interlude.wav
Features harmonic, pitched up X vocals meant to resemble a female's voice over a WILLIE G beat sampling "no, the moon" by Teen Suicide. X confirmed this song to be part two to "a message to tina belcher", which is evident in the spoken monlogue in the second half of the track where he speaks on his breakup and his pessimistic thoughts on love in general. Could be about Cinthia as there is a clear connection between her and her teeth in X's music. This appeared on multiple tracklists for "Heartbreak Hotel". Was re-released on all streaming platforms with its sample finally being cleared by Teen Suicide on June 19th 2024 after it was announced the day before.

Name:
DONDA - TRUE LOVE
(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. Kanye West, John Cunningham, Ojivolta & MIKE DEAN)
Notes: Re-released version of Kanye West and X's song "True Love" that features as the opening to the 2025 release of the 2022 album "Donda 2" which released with its title and song titles capitalized on April 29th 2025. The album would release onto YouTube and Tidal with it slowly releasing later onto places like Amazon, Spotify and finally Apple Music. This version of the song features much different mixing and new instrumentation that wasnt present in the previous release.

Name:
whoa (mind in awe) (Remix)
(feat. Juice WRLD) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: This track serves as the remix to X’s December 2018 posthumous release “whoa (mind in awe),” featuring Juice WRLD. Juice WRLD’s bridge and verse comes from a freestyle he did on the instrumental between March 19-20, 2019—and was later added to the track as a remix. Contrary to common belief, Juice WRLD was aware of this remix and created it during his lifetime, intending for it to be a remix, thus it is not a posthumous collaboration between two artists who did not want a song together. Prior to the release of this track, many Juice WRLD fans and even some X fans showed disappointment. Some felt that the label was exploiting Juice WRLD’s legacy, especially since his other posthumous project The Party Never Ends (Deluxe) was scheduled for release multiple times prior and never ended up releasing on time after many promises.

Name:
⭐ whoa (mind in awe) (Remix) [Upload 2]
(feat. Juice WRLD) (prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Second upload of the "whoa (mind in awe") remix that contains updated mixing on Juice WRLD's verses as fans were not pleased with the mix when the remix first released. The updated release would be shadow dropped June 5th, 2025 to avoid any potential controversies.

Name:
⭐ Juice WRLD - The Way
(with XXXTENTACION) (prod. John Cunningham)
(Inner Peace, Do You Know The Way?)
Notes: “The Way” is the second official collaboration between XXXTENTACION and Juice WRLD. Juice had stated that he planned to meet X before he passed away in June 2018, and they were already talking about collaborating. X’s estate gave Juice the opportunity to be on a couple of X’s songs, which (as we know of) include this one and a remix of “whoa (mind in awe).” On January 28, 2018, X shared a video of him singing the song on social media, likely before the original version had even been professionally recorded. His contributions were inspired by the then-popular Ugandan Knuckles meme and its associated phrase: "Do u kno da wae?" A demo version of the song leaked online on April 20, 2022, featuring vocals from Juice’s unreleased song known to fans as “Traveling” or “Inner Peace”. This version has better mixing and some lyrics from Juice are removed. Released July 11, 2025 as part of Juice WRLD's "Legends Never Die" 5 year anniversary Deluxe. This is the first time studio-recorded X vocals have been released officially since Bad Vibes Forever released 5 years, 9 months, and 5 days ago.

Name:

(feat. XXXTENTACION) (prod. YoungFlow305)
Notes:
OG Filename: IWatchedHimDrown.mp3
Re-release of the 2015 song "IWATCHEDHIMDROWN" by Ski Mask The Slump God that featured on his album "The Lost Files". the first Re-Release of IWATCHEDHIMDROWN by Ski Mask The Slump God was uploaded on all streaming platforms with many people believing the upload was fake as songs like "popit4daddy" was three seconds of silence and "Alien Sex" sounding like a low quality mp3. Ski would later confirm that the uploads were legit (but done poorly and released sooner than scheduled) and that more would be coming like "Freddy Vs. Jason" and more X collaborations.
